segments.text:
Behavior
The actions or reactions of persons or things under specified circumstances.
The genetic composition of complex human behaviors can be examined using Twin Studies
Twins show concordance if both have a trait and are discordant if only one twin has the trait.
Simple model systems can be used to determine the molecular/genetic component of simple behaviors

Characteristics of the fruitless gene
fruitless (fru) gene was first isolated in 1963
Includes several phenotypes, including male behavioral sterility
Encodes male-specific proteins, and female specific proteins
One effect of the mutation is to change the formation of serotonin producing cells, or the production of serotonin in those cells
Transcription factor
No obvious homolog in humans, but is conserved

wild-type
Control, normal splicing
Force male splicing
Gene constructs
Male version of mutant fruitless gene can be miss-expressed in same regions of both male and female brains

fruM female courting a wild-type virgin female
http://www.cell.com/supplemental/S0092-8674(05)00407-1

Females expressing the male version of fruitless, court females
Males expressing the female version of fruitless, do not court females

Vomeronasal (VNO) organ
Thought to mediate social behavior and neuroendocrine changes elicited by pheromonal cues
Main olfactory epithelium (MOE)
Small odorants

TRP2
Transient receptor potential
Ion channel protein-responds to stimuli
Localized to VNO sensory microvilli

Important Terms
Twin Studies
Mosaicism
Drosophila behavioral mutants
fruitless
Post mating female behavior
